summary.intent: Address a second batch of Codex review findings on PR #665 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering listbox/select selection behavior correctness, typeahead timing, description registration events, and select-all semantics.
summary.outcome: All five new Codex threads were fixed in a follow-up commit, CI passed (including proptests), threads were resolved with reply references to the commit SHA, and Codex review was retriggered.

repo: Selection behavior in listbox and select components lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/listbox/mod.rs and crates/ars-components/src/selection/select/mod.rs.
Typeahead uses current host time on std builds via a direct call; the `_at` variant is preserved as the deterministic adapter/test path; no-std keeps a monotonic fallback.
Space/Enter keydown dispatches SelectItem for replace-behavior mode and ToggleItem otherwise — the distinction is load-bearing for keyboard activation.
ExtendSelection must reject stale/non-selectable targets before scanning the range; missing target is a no-op, not an error.
SelectAll with Set::All semantics should preserve Set::All when at least one enabled option remains after toggling.
ItemActivated must guard on selectable-key before firing on_action, preventing structural/non-focusable collection nodes from triggering actions.
Description registration uses runtime events (on_description / has_description / description_attrs) wired through the component's event model.
The len-based pre-check in listbox select_plan was removed; the post-transition empty check is the sole decider for Set::All toggling.
Proptest suite for selection state machines is in crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines and runs with --ignored flag; PROPTEST_CASES=256 is the standard harness invocation.

code.finding: select_plan logic for Set::All toggle; len-based pre-check was removed so post-transition empty check governs.
ItemActivated handler; selectable-key guard added to prevent non-focusable/structural nodes from firing on_action.
SelectAll implementation; preserves Set::All when at least one enabled option exists.
Description registration event wiring (on_description, has_description, description_attrs).
Trigger/content keydown typeahead now uses current host time on std builds.
Select pointer selection path updated for behavior-aware dispatch.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via `gh pr comment`.
Review threads are resolved via GraphQL mutation resolveReviewThread using thread IDs fetched from the reviewThreads query.
Thread reply + resolve is scripted as shell functions (reply/resolve) using gh api to batch the six operations cleanly.
CI status and unresolved thread state are polled together in a single compound command after a sleep to avoid hammering the API.
Commit SHA is included in each resolve reply to make the fix traceable without cross-referencing the timeline.

The full local validation pipeline is: cargo +nightly fmt --all --check && cargo test -p ars-components selection && PROPTEST_CASES=256 cargo test --test proptest_state_machines selection -- --ignored && cargo check --features i18n && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

friction: Multiple fmt/clippy fix-and-rerun cycles were needed before the full suite was green, suggesting the patches were applied incrementally rather than in one shot.
CI polling required explicit sleeps (240s, 420s) because there was no event-driven notification for check completion.
Codex produced findings in batches across multiple review rounds, requiring repeated resolve/reply/push cycles rather than a single clean pass.
open_items: Codex was retriggered at end of session; a further review round may produce additional findings that need addressing.
Proptest suite runs with PROPTEST_CASES=256 locally but CI default may differ — worth aligning to catch regressions earlier.
Description registration event plumbing (on_description / has_description) was added but no explicit test coverage was mentioned — may warrant dedicated tests.
